title: New Tool
text: Adding new tool nemo child package consists following steps: Data Add sample files generated tool inst/extdata/<tool>. Usually top 10 rows (plus header) representative TSV file fine. DVC recommended tracking files version control. See vignette("devnotes") DVC notes. Schema Use config_prep_multi() + config_prep_write() generate skeleton schema.yaml sample files: functions auto-detect raw column names types; fill following fields generated YAML manually: tidy: column name tidy output (snake_case) description: field description (official tool documentation good source) versions: explicit array every tool version column appears (e.g. ['v1.2.3', 'latest']); use ['latest'] columns present current version. Save result inst/config/tools/<tool>/schema.yaml. See Structure details schema format Config reads . Parsers Create R6 class R/<Tool>.R. Use Tool1 (R/Tool1.R) template - demonstrates full pattern initialize(), per-table parse_*() / tidy_*() methods, roxygen documentation @testexamples blocks.
code: use("nemo") use("here", "here") use("glue", "glue") use("tibble", "tribble")  tool <- "mytool" d1 <- here(glue("inst/extdata/{tool}/latest"))  x <- tribble(   ~name     , ~descr                 , ~pat                        , ~type  , ~path                                       ,   "table1"  , "table1 description."  , "\\.mytool\\.table1\\.tsv$" , "txt"  , file.path(d1, "sampleA.mytool.table1.tsv")  ,   "table2"  , "table2 description."  , "\\.mytool\\.table2\\.csv$" , "csv"  , file.path(d1, "sampleA.mytool.table2.csv")  , )  out <- here(glue("inst/config/tools/{tool}/schema.yaml")) config_prep_write(config_prep_multi(x), out)

Hub - https://constructs.dev/ https://constructs.dev/packages/aws-cdk-github-oidc https://github.com/aripalo/aws-cdk-github-oidc Library https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/api/v2/docs/aws-construct-library.html L1: low level CFN Resources named CfnXyz e.g. CfnBucket L2: higher level convenient defaults e.g. s3.Bucket L3: patterns help common tasks involving multiple resources e.g. aws-apigateway.LambdaRestApi.

title: Structure
text: Config object reads single schema.yaml file inst/config/tools/<tool>/. table entry defines file pattern, file type, description, list columns — column carrying raw name (produced tool) tidy name (snake_case), type, description, versions array listing every tool version column appears . Config derives versioned raw tidy views unified source demand. See ?Config. format intro tables schemas tool schema.yaml inst/config/tools/<tool>/. flat tables map one entry per output file: ftype field signals file parsed: versions array column lists every tool version column appears , giving full picture additions removals: effective schema version V columns whose versions array contains V. schema_guess uses automatically match file’s columns correct versioned snapshot. Table-level metadata — patterns, file types, descriptions tables: Tool1 patterns. Tool1 file types. Tool1 descriptions. Per-table lookups: versioned raw tidy schemas: Per-table, per-version: csv-nohead-long tables, get_col_map() returns raw--tidy metric-name mapping used inside tool’s tidy_* method:
code: tables:   <table_name>:     description: '<human-readable description>'     pattern: "<regex matching the file basename>"     ftype: '<file type — see below>'     columns:       - raw: '<raw column name or positional label>'         tidy: '<tidy snake_case name>'         type: 'char | int | float'         description: '<human-readable description>'         versions: ['<version1>', '<version2>'] # table1 columns SampleID:   versions: ['v1.2.3', 'v4.5.6', 'latest']  # present in all versions metricX:    versions: ['v1.2.3']                        # dropped in v4.5.6 metricY:    versions: ['latest']                        # added in latest tool <- params$tool workflow <- params$workflow conf <- nemo::Config$new(tool, pkg = "nemo") conf ## #--- Config nemo::tool1 ---# ##  ## |var   |value | ## |:-----|:-----| ## |tool  |tool1 | ## |pkg   |nemo  | ## |ntbls |6     | conf$get_patterns()     |> knitr::kable(caption = glue("{tool} patterns.")) conf$get_ftypes()       |> knitr::kable(caption = glue("{tool} file types.")) conf$get_descriptions() |> knitr::kable(caption = glue("{tool} descriptions.")) conf$get_pattern("table1") ## [1] "\\.tool1\\.table1\\.tsv$" conf$get_ftype("table1") ## [1] "txt" conf$get_description("table1") ## [1] "Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited)." conf$get_schemas_raw()  |> dplyr::select("name", "version", "schema") ## # A tibble: 12 × 3 ##    name   version schema           ##    <chr>  <chr>   <list>           ##  1 table1 v1.2.3  <tibble [5 × 2]> ##  2 table1 v4.5.6  <tibble [4 × 2]> ##  3 table1 latest  <tibble [6 × 2]> ##  4 table2 v1.0.0  <tibble [2 × 2]> ##  5 table2 latest  <tibble [3 × 2]> ##  6 table3 v1.0.0  <tibble [3 × 2]> ##  7 table3 latest  <tibble [5 × 2]> ##  8 table4 v1.0.0  <tibble [3 × 2]> ##  9 table4 latest  <tibble [5 × 2]> ## 10 table6 v1.0.0  <tibble [3 × 2]> ## 11 table6 latest  <tibble [4 × 2]> ## 12 table5 latest  <tibble [4 × 2]> conf$get_schemas_tidy() |> dplyr::select("name", "version", "schema") ## # A tibble: 12 × 3 ##    name   version schema           ##    <chr>  <chr>   <list>           ##  1 table1 v1.2.3  <tibble [5 × 2]> ##  2 table1 v4.5.6  <tibble [4 × 2]> ##  3 table1 latest  <tibble [6 × 2]> ##  4 table2 v1.0.0  <tibble [2 × 2]> ##  5 table2 latest  <tibble [3 × 2]> ##  6 table3 v1.0.0  <tibble [3 × 2]> ##  7 table3 latest  <tibble [5 × 2]> ##  8 table4 v1.0.0  <tibble [3 × 2]> ##  9 table4 latest  <tibble [5 × 2]> ## 10 table6 v1.0.0  <tibble [3 × 2]> ## 11 table6 latest  <tibble [4 × 2]> ## 12 table5 latest  <tibble [4 × 2]> conf$get_schemas_both() ## # A tibble: 12 × 4 ##    name   tbl_description                                                           version schema   ##    <chr>  <chr>                                                                     <chr>   <list>   ##  1 table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    v1.2.3  <tibble> ##  2 table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    v4.5.6  <tibble> ##  3 table1 Table1 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    latest  <tibble> ##  4 table2 Table2 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    v1.0.0  <tibble> ##  5 table2 Table2 for tool1 (txt: header present, tab-delimited).                    latest  <tibble> ##  6 table3 Table3 for tool1 (txt-keyvalue: no header, 2 cols, col1=key col2=value).  v1.0.0  <tibble> ##  7 table3 Table3 for tool1 (txt-keyvalue: no header, 2 cols, col1=key col2=value).  latest  <tibble> ## 12 table5 Table5 for tool1 (csv-nohead-long: no header, long format with metric na… latest  <tibble> conf$get_schema_raw("table1", version = "v1.2.3") ## # A tibble: 5 × 3 ##   version field      type  ## 2 Mapped reads   reads_map   d     mapped reads   ## 3 Unmapped reads reads_unmap d     unmapped reads ## 4 Total bases    bases_total d     total bases

title: Structure
text: Tool main organisation class file parsers tidiers. contains functions parsing tidying typical CSV/TSV files (column names), TXT files column names missing. Currently utilises simple readr::read_delim function readr package reads data memory. See ?Tool. simple parsers used 80-90% cases, future can optimise parsing needed faster packages data.table, duckdb-r/duckplyr r-polars. initialise config list tidy filter write run can different Tool children classes inherit (override) functions fields Tool parent class. example, can create Tool object Tool1 follows: Initialise Tool1 object: Config object also constructed based name supplied - used internally find files interest infer schemas: can list files can parsed list_files(): can parse tidy files interest using tidy function. Note function called object assigned anywhere: can also focus subset files tidy using filter_files() function. include exclude arguments can specify tool_parsers include exclude analysis: tidying data interest, can write tidy tibbles various formats, like Apache Parquet, PostgreSQL, CSV/TSV R’s RDS. can see (optional) input_id specified added written files additional input_id column. can used e.g. distinguish results different samples data pipeline. optional output_id column can also added (e.g. distinguish runs input sample). input_prefix column can included via prefix_include = TRUE, use prefix file basename. run function convenient wrapper process filtering, tidying, writing.

title: Structure
text: Workflow consists list one Tools. can construct certain Workflow different Tools, allow parsing writing tidy tables variety bioinformatic tools. See ?Workflow. example, nemo contains Workflow1 class Workflow child (containing single Tool1 simplicity). Similarly Tool, Workflow object contains functions filter_files, list_files, tidy, write, run, get_tools() (access instantiated Tool objects):

title: Tidy Bioinformatic Pipeline Outputs
text: 📚 Docs: Installation | Files supported | Changelog | R6 structure | UML | CI/CD Bioinformatic pipelines produce lot output files, consuming downstream harder : Format variety: tools write TSV, CSV various proprietary formats, often mixed within pipeline Non-standard structure: files may transposed, headerless, embed section labels alongside data, requiring custom parsing logic tool Messy column names: raw names frequently uppercase, space-separated, dot-delimited, otherwise non-standard; joining across tools requires manual renaming Schema drift: column names file layouts change silently tool versions, breaking downstream code clear signal changed run-level provenance: hard tell output file came sample processing run files collected shared directory nemo R package attempts address issues providing schema-driven parsing tidying layer turns raw pipeline outputs consistently structured, versioned, analysis-ready tables. R6 classes (Tool, Workflow, Config) form base layer: given directory bioinformatic results, identify files YAML-defined schemas, reshape rename columns consistent tidy form, write specified format (Apache Parquet, TSV, CSV, RDS, PostgreSQL). run also produces metadata.parquet file alongside tidy tables, capturing IDs, paths, package versions. Downstream packages extend base classes supplying tool-specific schemas parsers. tidywigits tidydragen example R packages target large number outputs established bioinformatic pipelines WiGiTS/hmftools Illumina DRAGEN, respectively.

title: Tidy Bioinformatic Pipeline Outputs

title: Tool Object — Tool
text: Base R6 class nemo tools. Subclasses implement parsers specific bioinformatic tool outputs optionally overriding parse_{table_name}() tidy_{table_name}() methods custom parse tidy logic per table. Tool object: name (name); path directory outputs (path); schema configuration Config object (config); exposes matched files via list_files(); exposes parsed tidied tables via get_tbls(); typical workflow : optionally filter files filter_files(), parse tidy tidy(), write outputs write(). run() chains three steps.

title: Workflow Object — Workflow
text: Orchestrates multiple Tool objects shared directory, handling file discovery, filtering, tidying, writing single pipeline. Workflow object: name (name); one paths workflow results (path); holds Tool subclass objects accessible via get_tools(); exposes matched files across tools via list_files(); tibble written output files, populated write() (written_files); typical workflow : optionally filter filter_files(), tidy tidy(), write write(). run() chains three steps.
